alignments.preprint_claim_text: Higher organismal complexity correlates with lower fractal dimensions in both the structure and dynamics of the constituent proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Higher organismal complexity correlates with lower fractal dimensions in both the structure and dynamics of the constituent proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim with the same certainty and framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: As organismal complexity increases, the residue contact networks of the constituent proteins become more assortative. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: As organismal complexity increases, the residue contact networks of the constituent proteins become more assortative. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same but the publication reports it with higher certainty, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Statistical structural proximity across the proteomes may indirectly reflect the phylogenetic proximity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Statistical structural proximity across the proteomes may indirectly reflect the phylogenetic proximity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The statement appears unchanged in substance and certainty, with slightly different labeling as a conclusion in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The statistics of AlphaFold-predicted structures show that more complex organisms have larger radii of gyration, higher coil fractions, and slower vibrations in their proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present this specific set of structural statistics; instead it introduces a different broader conclusion, so this detailed preprint result appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification of missense variants as clinically significant or benign remains a major challenge despite advances in computational prediction scores. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not reiterate this explicit limitation statement, omitting this framing present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphScore, a novel AlphaFold2-based pathogenicity prediction score, was developed to distinguish between proxy-benign and proxy-pathogenic missense variants. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel AlphaFold2-based pathogenicity prediction score, termed AlphScore, was developed using engineered features from AlphaFold2 structures. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both texts describe the development of AlphScore; wording differs slightly but the contribution is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphScore alone showed lower performance than existing scores, such as CADD or REVEL. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphScore alone showed lower performance than existing in silico scores used for missense prediction, such as CADD or REVEL. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this performance comparison with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of AlphaFold2 predicted structures can improve pathogenicity prediction of missense variants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of AlphaFold2-predicted structures can improve pathogenicity prediction of missense variants. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same but the publication expresses it with higher certainty, reflecting amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When AlphScore was added to existing scores, the performance always increased as measured by deep mutational scan data and expert-curated missense variants from the ClinVar database. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: When AlphScore was added to existing scores, the performance increased as measured by the approximation of deep mutational scan data and the prediction of expert-curated missense variants from the ClinVar database. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the integrative performance improvement with the same evidence and certainty; wording is slightly different but meaning is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Thirty percent of high-risk bladder cancers recur despite current treatments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific quantified recurrence rate reported in the preprint is not present in the publication, so this concrete result appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified four consensus intrinsic subtypes of bladder cancer through transcriptomic profiling and targeted genomic sequencing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four subtypes of high-risk bladder cancer were identified, with an inflamed tumor subtype showing the highest responsiveness to BCG therapy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both texts report identification of four subtypes and highlight an inflamed subtype responsive to BCG; methods are implied in both.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A subtype of inflamed tumors with enhanced endogenous retroelement expression showed the highest responsiveness to BCG therapy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four subtypes of high-risk bladder cancer were identified, with an inflamed tumor subtype showing the highest responsiveness to BCG therapy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the finding that an inflamed subtype is most responsive but omits the specific mechanistic detail about endogenous retroelement expression, so the claim is presented in a clarified and narrower form.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning-based model was developed to predict recurrence risk with high accuracy (AUC = 0.90). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive model for recurrence risk achieved high accuracy with an area under the curve of 0.87. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a lower AUC (0.87 vs 0.90), so the model claim is preserved but quantitatively revised and the confidence in performance is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings establish a molecular precision framework for bladder cancer and nominate novel therapeutic targets. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert the strong translational claim about establishing a molecular precision framework or nominating targets; instead it notes limitations, so this broader conclusion appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neonate animals are evolutionarily prepared for generalisation, as evidenced by biases that do not depend on experience, reinforcement, or supervision. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Newborn chicks are evolutionarily prepared for generalization, via biases independent from experience, reinforcement, or supervision. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint conclusion about innate predispositions for generalization with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generalisation responses in neonate chicks showed biases such as asymmetrical generalisation gradients and faster learning for particular colours. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Generalization was inconsistent with an unbiased model, indicating the presence of biases in the learning process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claim the presence of biases in generalization; the publication frames it relative to an unbiased model but the substantive finding is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The chicks’ generalisation behaviour aligns with a Bayesian theoretical model that incorporates predispositions as initial preferences. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Generalization was consistent with a Bayesian model that incorporates predispositions as initial preferences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the Bayesian-model consistency with higher certainty and frames it as a result rather than a tentative conclusion, amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biases that facilitate generalisation are tuned to distinctive features that are unusual in the natural environment, such as the red and blue colours. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Biological and artificial intelligence can use biases tuned to infrequent stimuli to solve the problem of induction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint observation about biases for infrequent/distinctive features to a broader statement linking biological and artificial intelligence; thus the scope is broadened and the claim is clarified into a more general conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 3D-REMAP enables the discovery of marked drugs for dual-action agents that can both reduce the risk of heart failure and present anti-cancer activity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates the potential of structural systems pharmacology in designing polypharmacology for precision medicine.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's tool-level claim into a broader statement about structural systems pharmacology's potential; this clarifies and slightly broadens the claim rather than contradicting it.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Levosimendan, a drug for heart failure, inhibits serine/threonine-protein kinase RIOK1 and is active against multiple cancers, notably lymphoma.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Levosimendan is active against multiple cancers, notably lymphoma, through the direct inhibition of RIOK1 and RNA processing pathway.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a mechanistic detail (RNA processing pathway) while retaining the core inhibitory and activity claims; its lower stated certainty makes overall confidence appear tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that levosimendan can be a promising novel lead compound for the development of safe and effective multi-targeted cancer therapy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Levosimendan demonstrates anti-cancer activity for cancers that do not have effective treatment and reduces the risk of heart failure associated with existing anti-cancer therapies.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts from a cautious 'promising lead' statement to stronger efficacy and risk-reduction claims, revising and amplifying the practical implications relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 3D-REMAP demonstrates the potential of genome-wide multi-target screening in designing polypharmacology and drug repurposing for precision medicine.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates the potential of structural systems pharmacology in designing polypharmacology for precision medicine.
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases the preprint claim in the language of structural systems pharmacology but preserves the central conclusion about potential in designing polypharmacology.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Grouped model reliance is a model-agnostic permutation-based approach for interpreting trained decoding models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The present study demonstrates grouped model reliance as a model-agnostic permutation-based approach to interpret trained decoding models.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint contribution directly and with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both random forest and support vector machine models relied on alpha-band activity in most subjects during the Sternberg working memory task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results confirm that both random forest and support vector machine models relied on alpha-band activity in most subjects.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated with equivalent scope and certainty in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Patterns in frequency and particularly topography varied considerably between individuals, indicating pronounced inter-individual differences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Patterns in frequency and topography varied considerably between individuals, indicating more pronounced inter-individual differences than previously reported.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds comparison to previous reports, clarifying and slightly strengthening the contextual interpretation without changing the core observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Grouped model reliance provides a useful tool for understanding the workings of neural decoding models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Grouped model reliance indicates the extent to which a trained model relies on conceptually related groups of variables in EEG data.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a more specific description of what the tool indicates (extent of reliance on grouped variables), clarifying the original general usefulness claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Crossmodal congruence improved performance when both the attended modalities and the task irrelevant distractor were congruent.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Crossmodal congruence improved performance when both the attended modalities and the task-irrelevant distractor were congruent.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint result verbatim (minor punctuation difference only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incongruence between attended modalities led to impaired performance due to the congruence relation of the distractor to one of the attended modalities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: If the attended modalities were incongruent, the distractor impaired performance due to its congruence relation to one of the attended modalities.
alignments.rationale: The publication's wording matches the preprint's result exactly in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The magnitudes of crossmodal enhancement or impairment differed between attentional conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The magnitudes of crossmodal enhancement or impairment differed between attentional conditions.
alignments.rationale: Direct restatement with unchanged meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Largest crossmodal effects were observed in visual-tactile matching, with intermediate effects for audio-visual and smallest effects for audio-tactile matching.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The largest crossmodal effects were observed in visual-tactile matching, with intermediate effects for audio-visual and smallest effects for audio-tactile matching.
alignments.rationale: Identical finding presented in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Differences in crossmodal matching likely reflect characteristics of multisensory neural network architecture.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Differences in crossmodal matching likely reflect characteristics of multisensory neural network architecture.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We found 83 unannotated contigs exhibiting high identity with known tick viruses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We found 83 unannotated contigs exhibiting high identity with known tick viruses.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified five high-risk tick-borne viruses that have not been known to infect humans.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We found five high-risk tick-associated viruses that have not been known to infect humans.
alignments.rationale: Wording changed from 'tick-borne' to 'tick-associated' but the substantive claim about five previously non-human-known high-risk viruses is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two viral families, Nairoviridae and Phenuiviridae, contain a large proportion of potential zoonotic tick-borne viruses.
alignments.rationale: The publication's highlighted claims do not include the assertion about those two families containing a large proportion of potential zoonotic viruses, indicating this claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our analysis revealed 10 putative novel RNA viral contigs clustered with known tick-borne viruses.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present this specific '10 putative novel RNA contigs' claim among its stated results, so it appears removed from the final claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We advocate for a multidisciplinary approach within a One Health and EcoHealth framework to monitor ticks and the viruses they carry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study adds to the knowledge of tick virus diversity and highlights the importance of surveillance of newly emerging tick-associated diseases.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the recommendation for surveillance and multidisciplinary attention but frames it as highlighting surveillance importance rather than explicitly naming One Health/EcoHealth frameworks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tyrosine phosphorylated, lysine ubiquitinated, and lysine acetylated proteins were identified as part of the response to tyrosine kinase inhibitors in lung cancer cell lines.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis of large-scale post-translational modification data outlines cell signaling pathways affected by tyrosine kinase inhibitors in lung cancer cell lines.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the specific identification of modified proteins as an analysis that outlines affected signaling pathways, broadening the level of interpretation while preserving the underlying data-driven result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning was used to identify post-translational modification clusters that represent functional modules responding to tyrosine kinase inhibitors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning identified PTM clusters that represent functional modules responding to tyrosine kinase inhibitors.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents this methodological and result claim with essentially the same wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis revealed previously unappreciated connections between receptor tyrosine kinase signal transduction and oncogenic metabolic reprogramming in lung cancer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The constructed Pathway Crosstalk Network reveals connections between receptor tyrosine kinase signal transduction and oncogenic metabolic reprogramming in lung cancer.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the mechanism (Pathway Crosstalk Network) used to reveal the connections, clarifying the analytic basis while preserving the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Elucidation of crosstalk among signaling pathways employing different post-translational modifications reveals new potential drug targets for combination therapy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Elucidation of crosstalk among signaling pathways employing different post-translational modifications reveals new potential drug targets.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim about new potential drug targets but omits the explicit reference to 'combination therapy', narrowing the stated therapeutic implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Single sample pathway analysis (ssPA) enables the discovery of patient-specific pathway signatures from molecular level omics data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ssPA methods can facilitate pathway-based interpretation of metabolomics data.
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same conclusion that ssPA enables pathway-based interpretation of metabolomics/omics data at the single-sample level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GSEA-based and z-score methods outperformed other ssPA methods in terms of recall when applied to metabolomics data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GSEA-based and z-score methods outperformed other methods in terms of recall.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result about GSEA-based and z-score methods having superior recall.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clustering/dimensionality reduction-based methods provided higher precision at moderate-to-high effect sizes compared to other ssPA methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clustering/dimensionality reduction-based methods provided higher precision at moderate-to-high effect sizes.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same result regarding higher precision from clustering/dimensionality-reduction methods at moderate-to-high effect sizes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of ssPA to inflammatory bowel disease data demonstrates its ability to yield richer interpretations than conventional approaches.
alignments.rationale: No matching publication claim about the IBD application or the specific claim of yielding richer interpretations appears in the provided publication claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sspa python package provides implementations of all the ssPA methods benchmarked in this study.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed sspa python package provides implementations of all the methods benchmarked in this study.
alignments.rationale: Both assert the same contribution: an sspa Python package implementing all benchmarked methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine Learning for graphs can optimize drug phenotypic screens and predict compounds that mitigate abnormal brain reduction induced by excessive glial phagocytic activity in schizophrenia subjects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine Learning for graphs can optimize drug phenotypic screens and predict compounds that mitigate abnormal brain reduction in schizophrenia subjects.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level conclusion about applying graph ML to optimize screens and predict mitigating compounds.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method predicts a score associated with three possible compound effects: reduce, increase, or not influence phagocytosis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method predicts a score associated with three possible compound effects: reduce, increase, or not influence phagocytosis.
alignments.rationale: The result about the three-effect scoring prediction is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The high-throughput screening involving 2218 compounds at five different concentrations demonstrates good generalization capabilities of the method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The high-throughput screening involving 2218 compounds at five different concentrations demonstrates good generalization capabilities of the method.
alignments.rationale: Both versions present the same empirical result demonstrating generalization from the high-throughput screen.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A list of 64 compounds from the SWEETLEAD library has been identified as having potential clinical utility for glial phagocytosis mitigation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A list of 64 compounds from the SWEETLEAD library is provided, which have the most potential clinical utility for glial phagocytosis mitigation.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same list of 64 compounds and frames them as having clinical potential; wording is slightly stronger but the core claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study proposes a novel approach to computationally validate the utility of identified compounds as possible therapies for schizophrenia.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel approach is proposed to computationally validate the utility of the identified compounds as possible therapies for schizophrenia.
alignments.rationale: Both statements claim a novel computational validation approach for candidate compounds; content and certainty match.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clonal expansion of Salmonella strains in cattle was mainly influenced by the randomization of block and pen, as well as the origin/source of the cattle.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clonal expansion of Salmonella strains in cattle was mainly influenced by the randomization of block and pen, as well as the origin/source of the cattle.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same finding about the main influences on clonal expansion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Salmonella populations in cattle are highly clonal over a 6-month study period.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Salmonella populations in cattle are highly clonal over a 6-month study period.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication state the same result regarding clonality over 6 months.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified and compared the resistance genes, plasmids, and pathogenicity island profiles of the isolates within each sub-population.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified and compared the resistance genes, plasmids, and pathogenicity island profiles of Salmonella isolates within each subpopulation.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this methodological/result claim with equivalent detail about profiling resistance genes, plasmids, and pathogenicity islands.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There remains a gap in the literature concerning subpopulation dynamics within Salmonella serotypes in feedlot cattle from the beginning of feeding up until slaughter.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims emphasize importance of understanding subpopulation dynamics for mitigation rather than explicitly stating a literature gap as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Inactivity does not necessarily reflect negative welfare and can even be a sign of positive welfare, such as relaxation.
alignments.rationale: The publication claims do not restate this positive-welfare interpretation of inactivity; no closely matching publication claim is present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Inactivity Ethogram developed for fattening cattle includes detailed information on the postures of different body parts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study is the first to explore inactive behaviour in cattle by applying a detailed description of postures from an Inactivity Ethogram.
alignments.rationale: Both describe the ethogram contribution and emphasize the detailed posture descriptions; publication frames it as the first such study.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Husbandry system did not affect group level inactivity or the time the different postures were adopted, except for asymmetrical ears.
alignments.pub_claim_text: With increasing intensity of the husbandry system, more animals were inactive and the time the focal animals were inactive increased.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents an opposite result about husbandry intensity increasing inactivity, indicating a substantive revision of the preprint's finding rather than a simple clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Frequently co-occurring postures were generally similar between husbandry systems, but with subtle differences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The most frequently observed combination of postures on farms with intensive and semi-intensive systems was lying with head up, ears backwards and eyes open.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a specific most-frequent posture combination, narrowing the preprint's more general statement about similarities with subtle differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Inactivity Ethogram and the machine learning algorithm cspade are promising tools to distinguish between different affective states associated with inactivity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Inactivity Ethogram and the cspade algorithm may be valuable tools in future studies aiming to better understand different forms of inactivity and their association with different affective states.
alignments.rationale: Both claims align on the utility of the ethogram and cspade, but the publication frames the claim more cautiously ('may be valuable') compared to the preprint's stronger wording ('promising').

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cross-validation of the all-paths graph kernel shows slightly better results than the shallow linguistic kernel on the benchmark dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The all-paths graph kernel demonstrates slightly better performance than the shallow linguistic kernel on the benchmark dataset.
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same result that the all-paths graph kernel performs slightly better than the shallow linguistic kernel.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both models achieve state-of-the-art performance in the research area of relation extraction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both the all-paths graph kernel and the shallow linguistic kernel achieve state-of-the-art performance in relation extraction.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim that both kernels achieve state-of-the-art performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of shallow linguistic and all-paths graph kernel could further increase the overall performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of shallow linguistic and all-paths graph kernel could further increase overall performance slightly.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the preprint's suggestion by quantifying the expected improvement as slight, which is a clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The software for the tested kernels and the benchmark dataset is freely available for download.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new benchmark dataset of 2,613 sentences has been created for classifying functional relationships between proteins and small molecules.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly reports creation of a benchmark dataset (with size), but does not mention the software availability; the dataset aspect aligns while the software availability is absent, so the claim is narrowed/clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed pipeline can be easily up-scaled to process large datasets by applying the approach to 28 million abstracts.
alignments.rationale: No matching publication claim about up-scaling to very large corpora (28 million abstracts) appears in the provided publication claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lateral inhibition is associated with visual number sense in humans and animals. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Number sense can be produced under the influence of lateral inhibition and is independent of learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same association but frames it more strongly and adds independence from learning, increasing confidence relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number-sensing neural network model can reproduce the size and distance effects of the output response of human and animal number-sensing neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed number-sense neural network model can reproduce size and distance effects of output responses of number-selective neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result with equivalent wording and the same level of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number sense of the model disappears when lateral inhibition is removed. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Number sense can be produced under the influence of lateral inhibition and is independent of learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the role of lateral inhibition as producing number sense and does not explicitly state the removal consequence; the core idea is retained but presented more generally.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lateral inhibition strengthens the linear correlation between the total response intensity of the input layer and the number of objects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim describes the specific result about strengthening a linear correlation; this specific quantitative/model result appears omitted from the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lateral inhibition allows the output cells to prefer different numbers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed number-sense neural network model can reproduce size and distance effects of output responses of number-selective neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication describes number-selective neuron responses produced by the model but does not explicitly attribute tuning/preferences to lateral inhibition, so the preprint claim is presented in a less causal/explicit form.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fusion with a dynamically constrained model significantly improved estimation of lower-extremity kinematics over the video-only approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fusion with a dynamically constrained model significantly improved estimation of lower-extremity kinematics over the video-only approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dynamically constrained fusion improved estimation of joint kinematics and joint centers over unconstrained fusion when the quality of video data was high and that of inertial data was low. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dynamically constrained fusion improved estimation of joint kinematics and joint centers over unconstrained fusion when the quality of video data was high and that of inertial data was low. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim matches the preprint claim in wording, scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method consistently outperformed single-modality approaches across different noise profiles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method consistently outperformed single-modality approaches across different noise profiles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Complementary modalities and techniques can improve motion tracking by clinically meaningful margins. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Complementary modalities and techniques can improve motion tracking by clinically meaningful margins. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is carried over without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data quality and computational complexity must be considered when selecting the most appropriate method for a particular application. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Data quality and computational complexity must be considered when selecting the most appropriate method for a particular application. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this practical recommendation unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Increasing larval density led to testes and ejaculatory duct being overall larger in volume. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Increasing larval density led to testes and ejaculatory duct being overall larger in volume. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result text is the same but the publication reports it with reduced certainty, so the claim content is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The volume of accessory glands and, to a lesser extent, ejaculatory duct decreased with increasing larval density. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The volume of accessory glands and, to a lesser extent, ejaculatory duct decreased with increasing larval density. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim matches the preprint result but with lower reported confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two distinct classes of anatomical responses might reflect shared developmental regulation of the structures of the male reproductive system. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two distinct classes of anatomical responses might reflect shared developmental regulation of the structures of the male reproductive system. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The hypothesis is carried over unchanged in wording and scope, but the publication expresses it with lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Micro-CT can be an important tool to advance the study of anatomical (adaptive) responses to ecological environments. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Micro-CT can be an important tool to advance the study of anatomical adaptive responses to ecological environments. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The methodological contribution is reiterated in the publication with equivalent confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MCD-GAN outperformed other approaches in improving cross-scanner classification performance while preserving the anatomical layout of the original images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MCD-GAN outperformed other approaches in improving cross-scanner classification performance while preserving the anatomical layout of the original images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed MCD-GAN is the first generative model which incorporates downstream tasks while harmonizing. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed MCD-GAN is the first generative model which incorporates downstream tasks while harmonizing. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multi-site collaboration is essential for overcoming small-sample problems when exploring reproducible biomarkers in MRI studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multi-site collaboration is essential for overcoming small-sample problems when exploring reproducible biomarkers in MRI studies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved unchanged in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The semi-supervised gcWGAN generates more yields and covers 3.6 times more target folds compared to a competing data-driven method (cVAE). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gcWGAN model generates more successful designs and covers 3.5 times more target folds compared to a competing data-driven method (cVAE). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core result but slightly adjusts wording ("successful designs" vs "yields") and the reported fold-multiplier (3.6 -> 3.5), a small clarification rather than substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: gcWGAN designs have comparable or better fold accuracy and much more sequence diversity and novelty than cVAE. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: gcWGAN designs have comparable or better fold accuracy yet much more sequence diversity and novelty than cVAE. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same comparative performance and diversity claims with equivalent confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: gcWGAN explores uncharted sequence space to design proteins by learning from current sequence-structure data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No published claim asserts exploration of "uncharted sequence space"; the exploratory phrasing from the preprint is omitted in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ultra-fast data-driven model can be a powerful addition to principle-driven design methods through generating seed designs or tailoring sequence space. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ultrafast data-driven model boosts the success of a principle-driven de novo method (RosettaDesign) by generating design seeds and tailoring design space. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same idea but clarifies the target/practical impact (specifically boosting RosettaDesign), making the conclusion more concrete.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The R package sumrep efficiently performs a wide variety of repertoire summaries and comparisons.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim set does not mention the sumrep R package or the software contribution present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Summaries vary in their ability to differentiate between datasets, with many able to distinguish between covariates such as donor, timepoint, and cell type for BCR and TCR repertoires.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Summaries vary in their ability to differentiate between datasets, but many can distinguish between covariates such as donor, timepoint, and cell type for BCR and TCR repertoires.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same finding with very similar wording; the publication marks it as high certainty (amplified confidence relative to the preprint).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deletion and insertion lengths resulting from V(D)J recombination are more discriminative characterizations of a repertoire than summaries that describe the amino acid composition of the CDR3 region.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deletion and insertion lengths resulting from V(D)J recombination are more discriminative characterizations of a repertoire than summaries that describe the amino acid composition of the CDR3 region.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result essentially verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: State-of-the-art generative models excel at recapitulating gene usage and recombination statistics in a given experimental repertoire, but struggle to capture many physiochemical properties of real repertoires.
alignments.pub_claim_text: State-of-the-art generative models excel at recapitulating gene usage and recombination statistics in a given experimental repertoire, but struggle to capture many physiochemical properties of real repertoires.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same result with similar wording and the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Populations of neurons across V1, V4, and PIT responded over the full scene but focused on salient sub-regions within object outlines.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Populations of neurons with foveal receptive fields across V1/V2, V4, and PIT responded over the full scene but focused on salient subregions within object outlines.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the claim by specifying foveal receptive fields and adds V2; this narrows the population described while keeping the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best captured object feature class belonged to animals, not general objects, with this trend increasing over the visual hierarchy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neurons preferentially encoded animal features rather than general objects, with this trend strengthening along the visual hierarchy.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding with equivalent emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The monkey ventral stream is partially organized to encode local animal features over objects, even as early as primary visual cortex.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The monkey ventral stream is partially organized to encode local animal features over objects, even as early as primary visual cortex.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion appears verbatim in the publication with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different topic modeling methods can be effectively applied to gene expression data to compare their accuracy in reconstructing tissue structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Different topic modeling methods can be effectively applied to gene expression data to discover and reconstruct the tissue structure of the human transcriptome.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same core result about applying topic models to recover tissue structure with similar emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates the ability to distinguish healthy from cancerous tissues using topic modeling techniques on transcriptomic datasets.
alignments.rationale: No corresponding claim about distinguishing healthy versus cancerous tissues appears among the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The latent topic space recovered from transcriptomic data can serve as a useful embedding space for annotating transcriptomic profiles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The latent topic space serves as a useful low-dimensional embedding space for annotating transcriptomic profiles with high accuracy using a basic neural network classifier.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the embedding-annotation claim but adds classifier-based accuracy details, strengthening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper highlights the differences and pros and cons of various topic modeling methods across different tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the differences in properties of the latent space recovered by various topic modeling methods and their pros and cons across different tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this contribution, emphasizing latent-space properties and pros/cons across tasks with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel automated particle picking software crYOLO recognizes particles with high recall and precision after training on 500 to 2,500 particles per dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The particle picking software crYOLO automatically recognizes particles with high recall and precision after training with 200–2500 particles per dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication alters the numeric lower bound (200 vs 500), so the claim is revised while preserving the overall result about high recall/precision.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CrYOLO achieves a speed of up to five micrographs per second in particle recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: crYOLO reaches a speed of up to five micrographs per second.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the speed claim verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A general network trained on more than 40 datasets allows crYOLO to select previously unseen datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The general crYOLO network is able to pick from previously unseen datasets, enabling completely automated on-the-fly cryo-EM data preprocessing during data acquisition.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the general network's ability on unseen datasets and its implication for automation but omits the explicit "more than 40 datasets" training detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CrYOLO paves the way for completely automated 'on-the-fly' cryo-EM data pre-processing during data acquisition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The general crYOLO network is able to pick from previously unseen datasets, enabling completely automated on-the-fly cryo-EM data preprocessing during data acquisition.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's tentative conclusion is presented more strongly in the publication as an enabling result, increasing confidence and concreteness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Genetic associations with trabecular complexity were identified in 18,097 UK Biobank participants and replicated in an independently measured cohort of 1,129 healthy adults.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified 16 significant loci that contain genes associated with haemodynamic phenotypes and regulation of cytoskeletal arborization.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on the identified 16 loci and gene-level annotations, omitting the sample-size/replication detail stated in the preprint (thus revising and narrowing the presentation).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Genes in the associated regions are enriched for expression in the fetal heart or vasculature and implicate loci associated with haemodynamic phenotypes and developmental pathways.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified 16 significant loci that contain genes associated with haemodynamic phenotypes and regulation of cytoskeletal arborization.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes haemodynamic and cytoskeletal regulation links but does not mention the fetal-heart/vasculature expression or developmental pathway enrichment described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A causal relationship between increasing trabecular complexity and both ventricular performance and electrical activity is supported by biomechanical simulations and Mendelian randomisation studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Trabecular morphology is an important determinant of cardiac performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the link to cardiac performance but omits explicit mention of electrical activity and the detailed MR/simulation support, narrowing the causal focus to performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Myocardial trabeculae are a previously-unrecognised determinant of cardiovascular physiology in adult humans.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings suggest a previously unknown role for myocardial trabeculae in the function of the adult heart.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same conclusion but with somewhat lower stated certainty (medium vs high), tempering confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The open mzML format suffers from challenges such as large file sizes and a largely sequential I/O access pattern. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The open mzML format suffers from challenges such as large file sizes and a largely sequential I/O access pattern. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same limitation verbatim with the same level of certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Toffee is an open, random I/O format backed by HDF5 that achieves file sizes similar to the original vendor format with lossless compression. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Toffee is an open, random I/O format backed by HDF5 that achieves file sizes similar to the original vendor format with lossless compression. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated identically in the publication with the same claims about implementation and compression.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MzML and toffee are equivalent when processing data using OpenSWATH algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MzML and toffee are equivalent when processing data using OpenSWATH algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about equivalence in OpenSWATH processing is restated exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A peptide-centric deep-learning pipeline for peptide identification is proposed as a novel application enabled by new data access patterns. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A peptide-centric deep-learning pipeline for peptide identification is proposed as a novel application enabled by new data access patterns. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The proposed novel application is present in the publication with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Automatic differentiation can improve the parameterization of force fields used in molecular simulation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Automatic differentiation can improve the tuning of force fields used in molecular simulations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases 'parameterization' as 'tuning' but retains the same claim and certainty; wording is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned potential from the coarse-grained force field matches chemical knowledge and PDB data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned potential from the coarse-grained force field matches chemical knowledge and PDB data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned potential can fold and reproduce the dynamics of small proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned potential can fold and reproduce the dynamics of small proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are challenges in applying differentiable molecular simulation to all-atom models of proteins. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are challenges in applying differentiable molecular simulation to all-atom models of proteins. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is retained verbatim in the publication with the same uncertainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tumor microenvironment is highly associated with tumorigenesis through regulation of cellular physiology, signaling systems, and gene expression profiles of cancer cells. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on the CODA reconstruction method and specific 3D findings and does not restate this broad conclusion about tumor microenvironment association.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel method was established to reconstruct three-dimensional centimeter-scale tissues containing billions of cells from serially sectioned histological samples. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CODA demonstrates the ability to reconstruct three-dimensional (3D) distinct microanatomical structures in pancreas, skin, lung, and liver tissues. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the CODA method's reconstruction ability with concrete tissue examples; this clarifies the original methodological claim while keeping the same core contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning approaches were utilized to recognize eight distinct tissue subtypes from hematoxylin and eosin stained sections at micrometer and single-cell resolution. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CODA demonstrates the ability to reconstruct three-dimensional (3D) distinct microanatomical structures in pancreas, skin, lung, and liver tissues. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication describes reconstruction of microanatomical structures which likely incorporates the reported deep-learning tissue recognition, but the publication does not explicitly repeat the exact 'eight subtypes' or resolution details, so the match is plausible but less direct.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study maps in 3D modes of cancer invasion in the tumor microenvironment using samples from a range of normal, precancerous, and invasive pancreatic cancer tissue. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We show that pancreatic precancerous lesions develop into distinct 3D morphological phenotypes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a specific 3D finding about precancerous lesions' morphologies, which is a more specific/result-oriented version of the preprint's broader mapping claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a need for further 3D quantification of biological systems. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CODA allows the creation of readily quantifiable tissue volumes amenable to biological research. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's expressed need for further quantification is transformed in the publication into an asserted capability (CODA enabling quantifiable volumes), representing a substantive revision and increased confidence that quantification is feasible.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mutation D614G in the virus spike protein is unlikely to make changes in protein secondary structure and relative solvent accessibility. (certainty=low, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mutation D614G in the virus spike protein is unlikely to make changes in protein secondary structure and relative solvent accessibility. (certainty=low, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this specific result verbatim with the same low certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A spreadsheet dataset of point mutations based on 6,324 viral genome sequences can facilitate the investigation of SARS-CoV-2 in many perspectives. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A spreadsheet dataset of point mutations based on 6324 viral genome sequences can facilitate the investigation of SARS-CoV-2 in many perspectives. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same contribution (dataset) with identical scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Coding genes E, M, ORF6, ORF7a, ORF7b, and ORF10 are most stable and potentially suitable to be targeted for vaccine and drug development. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Coding genes E, M, ORF6, ORF7a, ORF7b, and ORF10 are most stable and potentially suitable to be targeted for vaccine and drug development. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about gene stability and targeting suitability with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hubble.2D6 predicts CYP2D6 haplotype functional status with 88% accuracy in a held out test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hubble.2D6 predicts CYP2D6 haplotype functional status with 88% accuracy in a held-out test set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The main performance result is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hubble.2D6 explains a significant amount of the variability in in vitro functional data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hubble.2D6 explains 47.5% of the variance in in vitro functional data among star alleles with unknown function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the quantitative amount of explained variance and limits the claim to star alleles with unknown function, clarifying and narrowing the original broader statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hubble.2D6 may be a useful tool for assigning function to haplotypes with uncurated function. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hubble.2D6 may be a useful tool for assigning function to haplotypes with uncurated function. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same cautious conclusion about the tool's potential utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Genetic variations in CYP2D6 are responsible for interindividual heterogeneity in drug response. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hubble.2D6 can be used for screening individuals who are at risk of being poor metabolizers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a practical application (screening poor metabolizers) that shifts the emphasis from the general biological conclusion to a specific clinical use; the mapping is plausible but not directly equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ERGO is a highly specific and generic TCR-peptide binding predictor that combines large-scale TCR-peptide dictionaries with deep learning methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ERGO is a highly specific and generic TCR-peptide binding predictor developed using NLP and deep learning methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution but specifies NLP as part of the method; this clarifies implementation details without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ERGO significantly outperforms current methods in tests for peptide-TCR binding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ERGO reaches similar results to state of the art methods in TCR-peptide binding tests.
alignments.rationale: The preprint claimed strong outperformance whereas the paper reports comparable performance, representing a clear tempering/revision of the original performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of peptide-TCR binding can be assessed through a defined set of standard tests.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate or discuss the asserted set of standard tests; the claim about defined standard tests appears omitted from the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The highest resolution cryo-EM structure of a BAR protein to date has been presented.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The highest resolution cryo-EM structure of a BAR protein has been presented.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution with no substantive change in wording or emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Endophilin B1 efficiently permeabilizes negatively charged liposomes that contain mitochondria-specific lipid cardiolipin.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Endophilin B1 efficiently permeabilizes negatively charged liposomes containing cardiolipin.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated identically in the publication with equivalent specificity and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new model for Bax-mediated cell death is proposed based on the findings related to endophilin B1.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new model for Bax-mediated cell death is proposed based on the findings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural networks can effectively sort particle species of different stoichiometries, revealing flexibility in BAR dimer organization and membrane deformation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural networks can effectively sort particle species of different stoichiometries, revealing flexibility in BAR dimer organization and membrane deformation.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this methodological/result claim verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STAligner enables spatially-aware data integration and simultaneous spatial domain identification across multiple ST datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: STAligner is a graph attention neural network designed for integrating and aligning spatial transcriptomics datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the model architecture (graph attention NN) and frames the same contribution more precisely; scope and confidence remain consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: STAligner efficiently captures shared tissue structures and disease-related substructures in various mouse and human cortex slices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: STAligner efficiently captures shared tissue structures, disease-related substructures, and dynamical changes during mouse embryonic development.
