alignments.preprint_claim_text: The shared spatial domain and nearest neighbor pairs identified by STAligner improve the accuracy of local structure-guided registration results compared to existing methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: STAligner achieves more accurate local structure-guided registration than existing methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative accuracy claim; wording differs but the meaning and scope are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Bacterial functional comparisons outperform marker genes in assigning taxonomic clades.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bacterial functional comparisons outperform marker genes in assigning taxonomic clades.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the result identically, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fusion-taxa are robust to the addition of novel organisms and can capture environment-driven bacterial diversity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fusion-taxa are robust to the addition of novel organisms and can capture environment-driven bacterial diversity.
alignments.rationale: The claim is restated with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The alignment-free nucleic acid-based Siamese Neural Network model enables finding shared functionality of very distant microbial homologs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The alignment-free nucleic acid-based Siamese Neural Network model enables finding shared functionality of very distant microbial homologs.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this methodological/result claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fusion profiles are better for assigning taxonomic clades than other functional annotation schemes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fusion profiles are better for assigning taxonomic clades than other functional annotation schemes.
alignments.rationale: The result is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Firing rate adaptation within a continuous attractor neural network causes the neural activity bump to oscillate around the external input, resembling theta sweeps of decoded position during locomotion.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Firing rate adaptation within a continuous attractor neural network causes the neural activity bump to oscillate around the external input, resembling theta sweeps of decoded position during locomotion.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this mechanistic result verbatim, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model explains the difference between 'bimodal cells' showing interleaved phase precession and procession, and 'unimodal cells' in which phase precession predominates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model explains the difference between ‘bimodal cells’ showing interleaved phase precession and procession, and ‘unimodal cells’ in which phase precession predominates.
alignments.rationale: The explanatory claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model accounts for the constant cycling of theta sweeps along different arms in a T-maze environment and the speed modulation of place cells’ firing frequency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model accounts for the constant cycling of theta sweeps along different arms in a T-maze environment and the speed modulation of place cells’ firing frequency.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains this combined explanation with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model explains the continued phase shift after transient silencing of the hippocampus.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model explains the continued phase shift after transient silencing of the hippocampus.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over identically to the final publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MinNet, a variation of the Siamese neural network framework, effectively integrates multi-omics data at single-cell resolution using graph-based contrastive loss.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MinNet demonstrates high accuracy and generalizability in integrating scRNA-seq with scATAC-seq and scRNA-seq with epitope data.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasizes the method's architectural details (Siamese variation, graph-based contrastive loss) while the publication emphasizes performance; the core integration claim is preserved but the technical framing changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrates accuracy and generalizability in integrating scRNA-seq with scATAC-seq and scRNA-seq with epitopes data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MinNet demonstrates high accuracy and generalizability in integrating scRNA-seq with scATAC-seq and scRNA-seq with epitope data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance claim for the same modalities with comparable certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MinNet uniquely removes the batch effect, a common problem in actual practice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model has a unique ability to remove the batch effect, which is a common problem in actual practice.
alignments.rationale: Both texts make the same strong claim about batch-effect removal and present it as a distinguishing property of the model.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of multi-omics data impacts downstream analysis, as validated by a model-based smoothing and cis-regulatory element inferring method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration results allow for analysis of the interplay between genome and transcriptome, aiding researchers in understanding their data.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses a more general benefit of integration (interplay analysis) compared with the preprint's specific validation methods, broadening the downstream-analysis claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework's application to a COVID-19 dataset highlights its necessity in single-cell multi-omics research.
alignments.rationale: The COVID-19-specific application and associated claim do not appear in the publication's summarized claims and thus appear to have been removed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: MinNet ranked top among other methods in benchmarking and is especially suitable for integrating datasets with batch and biological variances.
alignments.rationale: This benchmarking/ranking claim is present in the publication but has no counterpart in the provided preprint claims, indicating it was added.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study investigates the emergence of spatiotemporal patterns due to synthetic gene regulatory network motifs coupled with molecular diffusion.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study investigates the emergence of spatiotemporal patterns due to synthetic two- and three-node gene regulatory network motifs coupled with molecular diffusion.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies motif sizes (two- and three-node), narrowing the described motif set while preserving the overall investigative focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis reveals the impact of varying diffusion coefficients on the stability of patterns formed by different gene regulatory networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis reveals the impact of varying diffusion coefficients on the stability of patterns formed by gene regulatory networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same analysis and conclusion about diffusion coefficients and pattern stability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research provides insights into the design principles of pattern formation facilitated by gene regulatory network motifs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research offers insights into the design principles of pattern formation facilitated by gene regulatory network motifs.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about providing design-principle insights is preserved verbatim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study suggests mechanistic underpinnings of biological pattern formation through the coupling of multistable and oscillatory behaviors of gene networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest the mechanistic underpinnings of biological pattern formation.
alignments.rationale: The publication states a similar mechanistic-suggestion conclusion but omits the preprint's specific phrasing about multistable/oscillatory coupling, making the claim more general.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MuAt achieved prediction accuracy of 89% for whole genomes and 64% for whole exomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MuAt achieved prediction accuracy of 89% for whole genomes and 64% for whole exomes.
alignments.rationale: The numerical accuracy claims are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MuAt demonstrated a top-5 accuracy of 97% for whole genomes and 90% for whole exomes.
alignments.rationale: The top-5 accuracy figures are not represented among the publication claims provided and thus appear to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tumour representations learnt by MuAt included various tumour entities not used as training labels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MuAt is able to learn clinically and biologically relevant tumour entities without these tumour subtypes and subgroups being provided as training labels.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core claim about discovering tumour entities without labels but expresses it with higher certainty and emphasis on clinical/biological relevance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrated representations of somatic alterations hold significant potential to drive discovery of novel tumour entities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrated representations of somatic alterations learnt by MuAt were able to accurately identify histological tumour types and identify tumour entities.
alignments.rationale: The publication moves from a potential/aspirational statement to a demonstrated ability to identify tumour types, clarifying and strengthening the claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: MuAt models were found to be well-calibrated and perform well in three independent whole cancer genome cohorts with 10,361 tumours in total.
alignments.rationale: This validation/calibration claim across large cohorts appears in the publication but has no analogue in the provided preprint claims and is therefore an addition.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IT PlxnD1 and PT Fezf2 neurons showed distinct activation patterns during wakeful resting, spontaneous movements, and upon sensory stimulation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ITPlxnD1 and PTFezf2 neurons showed distinct activation patterns during wakeful resting, spontaneous movements, and sensory stimulation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about distinct activation patterns with effectively identical scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distinct IT PlxnD1 and PT Fezf2 subnetworks were dynamically tuned to different sensorimotor components of a naturalistic feeding behavior.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distinct ITPlxnD1 and PTFezf2 subnetworks were dynamically tuned to different sensorimotor components of a naturalistic feeding behavior.
alignments.rationale: The subnetworks' tuning to different sensorimotor components is preserved verbatim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Optogenetic inhibition of ITs PlxnD1 and PTs Fezf2 in subnetwork nodes disrupted distinct components of feeding behavior.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Optogenetic inhibition of ITPlxnD1 and PTFezf2 in subnetwork nodes disrupted distinct components of feeding behavior.
alignments.rationale: The causal disruption result via optogenetic inhibition is consistently reported in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dynamic areal and PN type-specific subnetworks are a key feature of cortical functional architecture linking microcircuit components with global brain networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dynamic areal and projection-neuron-type specific subnetworks are a key feature of cortical functional architecture.
alignments.rationale: The publication shortens the claim by removing explicit linkage to microcircuit-to-global-network integration; the conclusion is stated more conservatively (medium vs. high certainty).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Omnipose accurately segments samples that present significant challenges to current algorithms, including mixed bacterial cultures, antibiotic-treated cells, and cells of extended or branched morphology.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Omnipose is a deep neural network image-segmentation algorithm that accurately segments cells where current algorithms fail.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert accurate segmentation in cases where other algorithms fail; the publication summarizes this broadly but preserves the key result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Omnipose achieves generality and performance beyond leading algorithms and its predecessor, Cellpose, by virtue of unique neural network outputs such as the gradient of the distance field.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Omnipose achieves unprecedented segmentation performance on mixed bacterial cultures, antibiotic-treated cells, and cells of elongated or branched morphology.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms superior segmentation performance on the challenging sample types cited by the preprint; the methodological attribution is condensed but the performance claim stands.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Omnipose is a uniquely powerful tool for answering diverse questions in bacterial cell biology.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Omnipose is a powerful tool for characterizing diverse and arbitrarily shaped cell types from imaging data.
alignments.rationale: The publication broadens the claimed applicability from bacterial cell biology to diverse cell types and imaging contexts, expanding the scope of the tool's utility.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The benefits of Omnipose extend to non-bacterial subjects, varied imaging modalities, and three-dimensional objects.
alignments.rationale: This explicit extension to non-bacterial subjects, modalities, and 3D objects appears in the publication but was not present in the provided preprint claims, indicating an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Feedback-based motor control and motor adaptation may share a common implementation in primary motor cortex circuits. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Key features of trial-by-trial motor adaptation can arise from the internal properties of a recurrent neural circuit that adaptively controls its output based on ongoing feedback. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reformulates the preprint's idea in terms of internal properties of recurrent neural circuits governing feedback-based control and adaptation; this preserves the core claim but generalizes beyond explicit primary motor cortex phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A recurrent neural network trained with an error feedback signal can recover rapidly from external perturbations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The recurrent neural network was able to rapidly counteract external perturbations through an error-based feedback signal. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint result essentially verbatim about rapid recovery via error-based feedback in the RNN.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network learned to counteract persistent perturbations through a trial-by-trial process, reproducing key aspects of human adaptation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model natively reproduced several key aspects of behavioural studies in humans and monkeys. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims state the model reproduces key aspects of human/monkey behavioural adaptation through trial-like learning; the publication broadens species mention but preserves the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Resultant network activity changes were present in neural population recordings from monkey primary motor cortex. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network activity changes during learning matched those from populations of neurons from monkey primary motor cortex during the same task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly matches the preprint's observation that network activity changes align with monkey primary motor cortex recordings during the task.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hearing loss primarily impacts spectral processing, creating nonlinear distortions in cross-frequency interactions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hearing loss primarily impacts spectral processing, creating nonlinear distortions in cross-frequency interactions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint claim verbatim about spectral processing and cross-frequency distortions due to hearing loss.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These distortions result in a hypersensitivity to background noise that persists even after amplification with a hearing aid. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These distortions result in a hypersensitivity to background noise that persists even after amplification with a hearing aid. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model identifies hydrophobicity and shape complementarity as the primary determinants of peptide binding specificity in hA5. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Peptides exhibit multiple binding modes at the hA5 peptide interface, all of which have few polar contacts with hA5. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The work demonstrates how high-throughput experiments coupled with machine learning can reveal the determinants of binding specificity in low-specificity proteins. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: DL models with DE optimized hyperparameters exhibited less variation in predictive performance over repeated retraining compared to non-optimized DL models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result, with 'baseline' and 'non-optimized' used interchangeably; meaning and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models that explicitly incorporate pathway information in the form of a latent layer perform worse compared to models that incorporate this information implicitly. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models that explicitly incorporate pathway information in the form of a latent layer perform worse compared to models that incorporate this information implicitly. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In most evaluation setups, the best performance is achieved using a simple black-box model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: In most evaluation setups, the best performance was achieved using a black-box multilayer perceptron. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the type of black-box model (multilayer perceptron), clarifying the preprint's more general statement without changing scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Replacing the signaling pathways with randomly generated pathways shows comparable performance for the majority of these interpretable models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Replacing the signaling pathways with randomly generated pathways showed a comparable performance for the majority of the models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same experimental finding with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New interpretable models are necessary to improve the drug response prediction performance. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state this prescriptive conclusion; it instead reports performance results without asserting necessity for new interpretable models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The current study provides different baseline models and evaluation setups necessary for new models to demonstrate their superior prediction performance. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly claim to provide such baselines/setups as a primary contribution in the listed claims, so this preprint claim is not preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The seasonal influenza patterns in Australia and Chile showed a high correlation with those of the U.S. 22 weeks and 28 weeks earlier, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The seasonal influenza patterns in Australia and Chile showed a high correlation with those of the U.S. 22 weeks and 28 weeks earlier, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this correlation result identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The R 2 score of DNN models for ILI for the validation set in 2015–2019 was 0.722 despite the difficulty of forecasting 26 weeks ahead. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The R2 score of ANN models for ILI for the validation set in 2015-2019 was 0.758. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a different numeric R2 and refers to ANN rather than DNN; the core evaluated metric is the same but the reported result has been revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our prediction models forecast that the ILI for the U.S. in 2018–2019 may be later and less severe than those in 2017–2018. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prediction models forecast that the ILI for the U.S. in 2018-2019 may be later and less severe than those in 2017-2018. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is carried forward into the publication with the same content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The correlation for seasonal influenza among Australia, Chile, and the U.S. could be used to decide on influenza vaccine strategy six months ahead in the U.S. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The correlation between seasonal influenza patterns in the U.S., Australia, and Chile could be used to forecast the next seasonal influenza pattern. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication tones the claim down from a specific policy application (vaccine strategy) to a more general forecasting use, narrowing the practical implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A method for fully automated microfossil identification and extraction has been proposed and validated. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A method for fully automated microfossil identification and segmentation has been proposed and validated. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces 'extraction' with the more specific term 'segmentation', clarifying the nature of the automated method while preserving the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Deep Learning approach developed for microfossil identification achieved a high rate of correct identification at 98% Intersection Over Union. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Deep Learning approach developed for microfossil segmentation resulted in a high rate of correct segmentation at 98% IOU. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The metric and value are unchanged, but the publication frames the task as 'segmentation' rather than 'identification', a clarification of terminology.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The validation of the method was performed through automated quantitative analysis and visual inspection by specialists in micropaleontology. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication notes validation occurred but the listed publication claims do not preserve the specific description of validation methods (automated quantitative analysis and specialist visual inspection).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study presents the first fully annotated MicroCT-acquired publicly available microfossils dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper presents the first fully annotated MicroCT acquired microfossils dataset made publicly available. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this dataset contribution verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSimulator is a deep learning based simulator that mimics the entire pipeline of Nanopore sequencing. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepSimulator is a deep learning based simulator that mimics the entire pipeline of Nanopore sequencing. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The signals generated by the context-dependent model in DeepSimulator are more similar to experimentally obtained signals than those generated by the official context-independent pore model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The signals generated by the context-dependent model in DeepSimulator are more similar to experimentally obtained signals than those generated by the official context-independent pore model. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simulated reads produced by DeepSimulator can achieve accuracies ranging from 83% to 97%, with the default parameters yielding reads that have almost the same properties as real data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simulated reads produced by DeepSimulator can achieve different accuracies ranging from 83 to 97%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two case studies demonstrate that DeepSimulator can benefit the development of tools in de novo assembly and low coverage SNP detection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepSimulator has applications in the development of tools for de novo assembly and low coverage SNP detection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepWAS is a new approach that directly integrates predictions of regulatory effects of single variants into a multivariate GWAS setting. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepWAS integrates regulatory effect predictions of single variants into a multivariate GWAS setting. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological contribution with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Up to 40,000 regulatory single-nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) were associated with multiple sclerosis, major depressive disorder, and height, identifying 43-61 regulatory SNPs, called deepSNPs, that reach at least nominal significance in large GWAS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Up to 61 regulatory SNPs, called dSNPs, were associated with multiple sclerosis, major depressive disorder, and height. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the central result about identified regulatory SNPs but simplifies and narrows the numeric claim to 'up to 61', clarifying the earlier phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepSNPs are enriched in public or cohort-matched expression and methylation quantitative trait loci. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: dSNPs were enriched in public or cohort-matched expression and methylation quantitative trait loci. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The enrichment result is carried over with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepWAS has the potential to directly generate testable functional hypotheses based on genotype data alone. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepWAS has the potential to generate testable functional hypotheses based on genotype data alone. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same concluding potential without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepWAS can identify individual SNPs in non-coding regions with gene regulatory capacity that contribute to disease risk. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Up to 61 regulatory SNPs, called dSNPs, were associated with multiple sclerosis, major depressive disorder, and height. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication documents identified regulatory SNPs associated with disease, which supports the preprint's capability claim but presents it as specific associated dSNPs rather than a broader capability statement.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The prediction accuracy was higher for DeepWAS than for classical GWAS models for 91% of the genome-wide significant, MS-specific dSNPs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific comparative accuracy result does not appear in the preprint and is newly reported in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A-Prot predicts long-range contacts better than the existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A-Prot predicts long-range contacts better than existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same performance claim about long-range contact prediction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The A-Prot models are more accurate than most top server groups of CASP14. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The A-Prot models are more accurate than most top server groups of CASP14. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This benchmark claim is reiterated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A-Prot captures evolutionary and structural information of proteins accurately with relatively low computational cost. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A-Prot accurately captures the evolutionary and structural information of proteins with relatively low computational cost. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about representational accuracy and efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A-Prot can provide a clue for the development of other protein property prediction methods. (certainty=low, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Developing a lighter prediction model is necessary despite the success of AlphaFold. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed bidirectional recurrent model of attention can recognize and segment simple stimuli such as digits as well as objects in natural images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model successfully performs canonical attention tasks of orienting, filtering, and visual search on complex scenes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can learn to perform a range of behavioral findings, including object binding, selective attention, inhibition of return, and visual search. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model replicates key psychophysical phenomena, such as perceptual load and inattentional blindness. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model replicates a variety of neural findings, including increased activity for attended objects, features, and locations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model unifies decades of cognitive and neurophysiological findings of visual attention into a single principled architecture. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed bidirectional recurrent gating mechanism integrates into a principled architecture of the ventral visual stream. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ability to selectively and dynamically focus on specific parts of stimulus streams can help artificial neural networks to better generalize and align with human brains. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work provides evidence that attentional and binding phenomena can emerge from error-backpropagation combined with architectural constraints upon information flow. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: PoseR can classify animal behaviour quickly and accurately using model organisms such as zebrafish larvae, Drosophila melanogaster, mice, and rats. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The tool simplifies the behavioural analysis workflow by transforming coordinates of animal position and pose into semantic labels with speed and precision. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: PoseR ensures scalability and versatility for use across multiple species and contexts, improving the efficiency of behavioural analysis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive models trained on known causal genes in Arabidopsis and rice could recall about 64% and 79% of causal genes, respectively, when the top 20% ranked genes were considered. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Several important features of causal genes were identified, including paralog copy number, being a transporter, being a transcription factor, and containing SNPs that cause premature stop codon. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: This work lays the foundation for systematically understanding characteristics of causal genes and establishes a pipeline to predict causal genes based on public data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Employing multiple Z tests with a machine-learning algorithm provides a better prediction on NIPT data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained model achieved 100% accuracy on all three chromosomes in 4752 qualified NIPT data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained SVM models achieved 100% accuracies in both internal validations and unknown sample predictions for screenings of 13-/18-/21-trisomies.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result (100% accuracies for trisomies 13/18/21) and thus preserves the original performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Four false positives and four false negatives were corrected by using the machine-learning model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four false positives and four false negatives caused by Z-test were corrected by using the SVM models.
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the same correction of four false positives and four false negatives by the SVM; the publication only adds that they were caused by the Z-test.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first study to employ support vector machine in NIPT data analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study is one of the earliest to employ SVM in NIPT NGS data analysis, and it is expected to replace Z-test in clinical practice.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method is expected to replace the current one-Z-value based NIPT analysis in clinical use.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study is one of the earliest to employ SVM in NIPT NGS data analysis, and it is expected to replace Z-test in clinical practice.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Other machine learning models can also achieve similar high accuracy in NIPT NGS data analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a broader claim that other ML models can also attain similar performance; this was not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The SVM model is the most robust among the models tested in this study.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PrefixProt is a framework for controllable protein design that employs prefix tuning to learn virtual tokens as control tags.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PrefixProt is a framework for controllable protein design that employs prefix-tuning to learn virtual tokens as control tags.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of PrefixProt was validated through extensive experiments encompassing both protein structure design and protein function design.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Benchmark results demonstrate that prefix virtual tokens efficiently guide the pre-trained ProtLM by optimizing a smaller number of trainable parameters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Benchmark results demonstrate that prefix virtual tokens efficiently guide the pretrained ProtLM by optimizing a smaller number of trainable parameters.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The compositional flexibility of virtual tokens facilitates the generation of proteins with multiple target properties.

alignments.pub_claim_text: PrefixProt outperforms other parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods and text-guided ProtLMs, particularly in scenarios with limited data availability.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a comparative performance claim (outperforming alternatives and data-scarce scenarios) that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: PrefixProt establishes a robust framework for de novo protein design with promising applications in drug discovery and biomedicine.
alignments.rationale: The publication extends the preprint by highlighting translational applications and framing PrefixProt as a robust de novo design framework, which is new content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The G enomic P re-trained N etwork (GPN) successfully learns gene structure and DNA motifs without any supervision.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Genomic Pre-trained Network (GPN) successfully learns gene structure and DNA motifs without any supervision.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPN outperforms predictors based on popular conservation scores such as phyloP and phastCons in predicting the functional impact of genetic variants in Arabidopsis thaliana.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GPN outperforms predictors based on popular conservation scores such as phyloP and phastCons.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPN is designed to learn genome-wide variant effects through unsupervised pre-training on genomic DNA sequences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model is designed to learn genome-wide variant effects through unsupervised pretraining on genomic DNA sequences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictions made by GPN for Arabidopsis thaliana can be visualized as sequence logos in the UCSC Genome Browser.

alignments.pub_claim_text: GPN is capable of predicting the functional impact of genetic variants in Arabidopsis thaliana using allele frequencies from the 1001 Genomes Project.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed representations of variable-order Markov models take up to 4 times less space than previous implementations based on the suffix array.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed representations of variable-order Markov models take up to four times less space than previous implementations based on the suffix array.

alignments.pub_claim_text: By exploiting constraints on the length and frequency of contexts, the compressed indexes can achieve sizes that are a hundred times smaller than previous implementations based on the suffix array.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the extreme compression claim (100x smaller) consistent with the preprint's result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The advancements allow variable-order Markov models to be trained on bigger datasets and with longer contexts on the same hardware.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The advancements allow variable-order Markov models to be used with bigger datasets and longer contexts on the same hardware.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the conclusion about enabling use/training on larger datasets and longer contexts; wording differs minimally.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new gated neighborhood-modulating graph transformer called DProQA was introduced to predict the quality of 3D protein complex structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new gated neighborhood-modulating graph transformer called DProQA was introduced to predict the quality of 3D protein complex structures.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DProQA was ranked 3rd among single-model quality assessment methods in the 2022 CASP15 experiment based on the ranking loss of TM-score on 36 complex targets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DProQA was ranked 3rd among the single-model quality assessment methods in the 2022 CASP15 experiment based on the ranking loss of TM-score on 36 complex targets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The rigorous internal and external experiments demonstrate that DProQA is effective in ranking protein complex structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The rigorous internal and external experiments demonstrate that DProQA is effective in ranking protein complex structures.
alignments.rationale: The publication duplicates the conclusion about experimental effectiveness directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach reduces the generalization MAE by 44% in the Alzheimer’s Disease Neuroimaging Initiative dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach reduces the generalization MAE by 47% (from 5.25 to 2.79 years) in the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We achieve a 29% reduction in scan-rescan error while enhancing the model’s robustness to registration errors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieve up to 13% reduction in scan-rescan error (from 0.80 to 0.70 years) while enhancing the model's robustness to registration errors. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High-quality preprocessing and robust training techniques are critical for improving accuracy and narrowing the generalization gap. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These results highlight the critical role of high-quality preprocessing and robust training techniques in improving accuracy and narrowing the generalization gap. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our study makes valuable contributions to neuroimaging research by offering a potential pathway to improve the clinical applicability of deep learning models. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictive model achieved a mean squared error (MSE) of 0.6, a mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of 10%, and a mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.5. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive model achieved a mean squared error (MSE) of 0.6, a mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of 10%, and a mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.5. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identified 128 top natural compounds based on their predicted bioactivity against TNF-α. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identified 128 top natural compounds based on their predicted bioactivity against TNF-α. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Four compounds were found to pose nominal toxicity after evaluation using molecular docking and ADMET analysis. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Four compounds were found to pose nominal toxicity after evaluation using molecular docking and ADMET analysis. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The toxicity evaluation result is repeated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Imperialine, Veratramine, and Gelsemine are proven to be potential natural inhibitors of TNF-α. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Imperialine, Veratramine, and Gelsemine are proven to be potential natural inhibitors of TNF-α based on total binding free energy estimates. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep learning-based super-resolution approach can generate high-resolution isotropic T2-weighted scans from low-resolution paediatric input scans. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A multi‐orientation U‐Net was trained to generate high‐resolution scans from multiple low‐resolution inputs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert generation of high-resolution scans from low-resolution inputs, but the publication specifies a multi-orientation U‑Net and multiple inputs and omits mention of paediatric isotropic T2, so the methodological/description details were revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach exhibits improved quality of outputs compared to current state-of-the-art methods for super-resolution of ultra-low-field scans in paediatric populations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present an explicit claim that the approach outperforms current state-of-the-art methods for paediatric ultra-low-field scans, so this comparative claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model improves volume estimates of the caudate with a linear correlation of r = 0.94, compared to 0.84 using existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific quantitative caudate volume-estimate improvement and comparison to existing methods do not appear in the publication claims, indicating removal of this specific result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research serves as proof-of-principle of the viability of training deep-learning based super-resolution models for use in neurodevelopmental research. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Enhancing the quality of ultra‐low‐field scans is crucial for neurodevelopmental studies in low‐income settings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the proof-of-principle claim as emphasis on the importance of scan enhancement specifically for neurodevelopmental studies in low-income settings; this shifts focus and narrows the scope.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The cost of modern MRI systems limits their accessibility in low‐income settings. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This conclusion about MRI cost and accessibility is present in the publication but has no counterpart in the preprint claims, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artificial intelligence methods for constructing structural models of proteins are transforming biomolecular sciences. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on AlphaFill and related technical results and does not explicitly restate the broad transformational claim, so this high-level statement from the preprint is not present among publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AlphaFold protein structure database provides hundreds of thousands of protein structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A total of 12,029,789 transplants were performed on 995,411 AlphaFold models and are available in the alphafill.eu databank. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication supplies precise counts and resources, clarifying and quantifying the preprint's statement about the large AlphaFold model database.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AlphaFill is an algorithm that can transplant missing small molecules and ions from experimentally determined structures to predicted protein models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaFill is an algorithm that can 'transplant' missing small molecules and ions from experimentally determined structures to predicted protein models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim about AlphaFill's transplantation capability is restated in the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The structural annotations provided by AlphaFill are intended to help scientists interpret biological function and design experiments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AlphaFill algorithm was successfully validated against experimental structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds validation evidence for AlphaFill, which strengthens and clarifies the utility of its structural annotations for interpretation and experiment design, increasing confidence in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The structure of mRNA in vivo is significantly differentiated from that in vitro due to various factors involved in the translation process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structure of mRNA in vivo is unwound to some extent in response to multiple factors involved in the translation process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the characterization (specifies 'unwound to some extent') while preserving the same overall result about translation-related differences between in vivo and in vitro structure.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ribosome density of the structural region is the most important factor affecting the structural stability of mRNA in vivo. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Translation initiation ribosome density (INI) is the most critical factor affecting the structure of mRNA in vivo. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the ribosome density measure (INI) as the critical factor, clarifying the preprint's more general ribosome-density claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The strength of the mRNA structure in vitro has a relatively small effect on its structural stability in vivo. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Structural sequence features, including minimum folding free energy (MFE) and GC content, have relatively smaller effects on mRNA structure in vivo. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies which in vitro sequence features have smaller effects, clarifying the preprint's more general statement about in vitro strength having limited effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep neural network model can predict the structural stability of mRNA in vivo by fitting six quantifiable features. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepDRU predicts the degree of mRNA structure unwinding in vivo by fitting five quantifiable features that may affect mRNA folding. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the predictive model (DeepDRU) but reports five fitted features rather than six and frames prediction as unwinding degree, so the model claim was revised in detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study provides improved knowledge on the mechanisms of factors influencing mRNA structural stability, facilitating the design and functional analysis of mRNA structure in vivo. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepDRU provides a new paradigm for predicting the unwinding capability of mRNA structure in vivo. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution as a new predictive paradigm (DeepDRU) for unwinding capability, clarifying the study's practical contribution to design and analysis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The heterogeneity of firing rate and types of firing patterns make a greater contribution than chaos to the steepness of the synchronization transition curve. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The heterogeneity of firing rates and firing patterns makes a greater contribution than chaos to the steepness of the synchronization transition curve. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording is effectively identical and the meaning is the same; no change in scope or confidence is evident.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Chaotic dynamics of the isolated neurons do not always make a visible difference in the process of network synchronization transitions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Chaotic dynamics of the isolated neurons do not always make a visible difference in the transition to full synchrony. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Macroscopic chaos is observed regardless of the dynamics nature of the neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Macroscopic chaos is observed regardless of the dynamics nature of the neurons. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Chaotic nodes can promote multi-stable behaviour, where the network dynamically switches between a number of different semi-synchronized, metastable states. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model supports stationary and travelling bumps of localised activity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model supports stationary and travelling bumps of localised activity. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: We construct bumps and travelling waves analytically in a purely deterministic version of the model. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Stationary and travelling bumps have similar meso- and macroscopic profiles but different microscopic structures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Both MCF10A and HL60 cells exhibit reproducible guidance of actin waves on periodic surface topographies. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The underlying actin guidance by nanotopography is similar in both MCF10A and HL60 cells at the micron and sub-micron scales. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The underlying actin guidance by nanotopography is similar in both MCF10A and HL60 cells at the micron and submicron scales. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pharmacological inactivation of the medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC) enhanced reward-seeking choice under the risk of positive punishment. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The behavioral change observed was well-explained by hypersensitivity to the reward, rather than a decrease in the strength of aversion to punishment. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mPFC suppresses reward-seeking behavior by reducing sensitivity to reward under the risk of positive punishment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mPFC suppresses reward-seeking behavior by reducing sensitivity to reward under the risk of positive punishment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is repeated verbatim with the same expressed certainty, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Conditioned approach was attenuated for the cue-reward contingency that was degraded. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Conditioned responding was attenuated for the cue-reward contingency that was degraded. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dopamine neuron activity in the midbrain and dopamine release in the ventral striatum showed a profile of changes in cue- and reward-evoked responding that was not easily explained by a standard reinforcement learning model. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An alternative model based on learning causal relationships was better able to capture evoked dopamine responses during contingency degradation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An alternative model based on learning causal relationships better captured dopamine responses during contingency degradation compared to a standard reinforcement learning model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the alternative causal-learning model better accounts for dopamine responses, with equivalent comparison and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results suggest that mesostriatal dopamine encodes the contingencies between meaningful events during learning. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mesostriatal dopamine encodes the contingencies between meaningful events during learning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is the same but the publication expresses it with higher certainty (low → medium), so the claim's confidence was amplified.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Contingency degradation abolished the trial-by-trial history dependence of the dopamine responses at the time of trial outcome. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The brain may have evolved a modular architecture for reward-based learning that enables better learning and generalization than architectures with less specialized modules. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An architecture that segregates computations of state representation, value, and action into specialized modules enables more efficient learning and better generalization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modular agent achieved better learning and generalization compared to agents with less specialized modules. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Behaviors of agents with a modular architecture resemble macaque behaviors more closely. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The behavior of the modular agent resembles macaques' behavior more closely than that of other agents. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned state representation in these agents combines prediction and observation, weighted by their relative uncertainty, similar to a Kalman filter. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned state representation of the modular agent combines prediction and observation, weighted by their relative uncertainty. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same technical result about the learned state representation with essentially identical meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artificial systems can use insights from neuroscience to improve learning and generalization in natural tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings suggest that artificial systems can improve learning and generalization in natural tasks by adopting insights from neuroscience. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The standard actor-critic model of birdsong learning fails to explain juvenile zebra finches' efficient learning of multiple syllables. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Replacing a single actor with multiple independent actors that jointly maximize a common intrinsic reward accurately reproduces birds' empirical learning trajectories. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The influence of each actor on the magnitude of global reward is competitively determined by its acoustic similarity to target syllables. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VTA dopamine neurons imbue actions and their associated cues with motivational value that allows flexible and persistent pursuit. (certainty=high, type=result)
